text:
Jakob Ingebrigtsensent a classy message toBritain's Josh Kerrafter failing to secure a medal in theOlympic 1500m final. The rivalry between the 23-year-old andTeam GB'sKerr had been simmering since the 2023 World Championships in Budapest, where Kerr shocked the athletics world by beating Ingebrigtsen in the 1500m final.

Despite Ingebrigtsen's victory over Kerr in their initial heat last year, it was the 26-year-old Scot who triumphed when it counted, clinching the title in the final. Kerr's winning time of 3:29.38 edged out Ingebrigtsen for the gold at the championships by a mere 0.27 seconds, leaving the Norwegian competitor disappointed.

Post-race comments from the 2023 event saw Ingebrigtsen cite illness for his lacklustre performance and boldly claim he could beat Kerr "blindfolded". Kerr didn't take kindly to the remarks, retorting: "I'm like, 'You've won so many races, you ran fantastic all season that you're just on this - you must be surrounded by so many yes-men that you don't realise that you have weaknesses'. And I think that was part of his downfall."

He even egged on Ingebrigtsen, saying: "I would love for him to be listening to this," reportsthe Mirror. Anticipation was high as the two lined up for the1500m showdownat theParis Olympicson Tuesday - and while Kerr didn't manage to grab the gold, he did succeed in besting Ingebrigtsen, who finished outside the medal positions.

USA star Cole Hockerstormed through the competition to set an impressive Olympic record time of 3:27.65 in the final, with Kerr hot on his heels finishing second, just ahead of third-placeYared Nuguseby a mere 0.01 seconds. Ingebrigtsen was left trailing in fourth after missing the mark tactically, a stark contrast to hisTokyotriumph three years prior.

In a gracious display of sportsmanship following the upset, Ingebrigtsen extended an olive branch online, succumbing to humility after his failure to secure a spot on the podium, even as his rival Kerr elevated his bronze from Tokyo 2021 to silver.



Discovery+is the new home of the Olympics. The streamer is set to broadcast 3,800 hours of sport live from Paris.

Sports fans can getDiscovery+ for £3.99a month until the end of 2024 via Amazon Prime's video channels. This also comes with anAmazon Prime 30-day free trial.

OnInstagram, he wrote: "Well, I guess he did show up after all...," in a nod to Kerr, before adding: "Hocker and Nuguse as well. My team always say that 'because you have a big mouth and is the one to beat, you have everything to lose in competitions'. Today, Cole Hocker, Yared Nuguse and Josh Kerr outsmarted me."

Celebrating the prowess of his competitors, he admitted: "They were 'the best guys' when it really mattered. And I want to congratulate them all on a great performance! Of course, I am disappointed! Running is the ultimate sport. Everyone can participate. Everyone can set their own goals and achieve them. All you need is to show up."

Reflecting on his journey, Ingebrigtsen shared: "Not long ago, I trained alongside kids and average runners at Sandnes stadium. Today, I competed alongside one of my countrymen in an Olympic 1500 final. Ten years ago, no one in Norway would have believed this was possible. Until Henrik and Filip showed us all that it is."



We use your sign-up to provide content in ways you've consented to and to improve our understanding of you. This may include adverts from us and 3rd parties based on our understanding. You can unsubscribe at any time. Read ourPrivacy Policy

Ingebrigtsen also extended heartfelt thanks to his inner circle and backers, lauding the role of his fellow competitors in pushing him to greatness. The grateful athlete went on: "I sincerely want to express my gratitude and love to them and the rest of the team, and to my partners and the Norwegian athletics association and all my followers for the support this year.

"Thank you so much. It means a lot to me! Thank you also to all my competitors for putting on a great show today. Congratulations to the guys bringing home the medals! Luckily for me, I get to do it all again tomorrow morning."

title: Jakob Ingebrigtsen's classy message to Josh Kerr after 1500m defeat | Other | Sport | Express.co.uk
author: Aaron Morris
description: Josh Kerr and Jakob Ingebrigtsen have been embroiled in a war of words in recent years, but the Norwegian was full of praise for his rival after the 1500m final
